response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which makes 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries they collected is 76.

***

They used their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make they divided the total number of blueberries by 9. This calculation gave them 8 batches with 4 blueberries remaining. They sold each batch for $5. So they earned 8 times 5 dollars from the muffins.

***

The remaining 4 blueberries were not used for muffins. They decided to keep them for future use. The money they made from selling the muffins was 40 dollars. This amount represented the earnings from their blueberry collection. The process of collecting and selling the blueberries provided them with a sense of accomplishment.

***

Their efforts showed the value of teamwork and planning. The money they made from the blueberries they collected was 40 dollars. This amount was a result of their combined efforts and the successful sale of the muffins. The youth of the group was evident in their enthusiasm and dedication to the task.
